definition: The saved settings MySQL Workbench uses to connect to a MySQL server.
An organized collection of data managed by MySQL.
A column, or group of columns, that uniquely identifies each row in a table.
A SQL command used to read, insert, update, or delete data.
A named database container in MySQL that stores tables, views, and other database objects.
A structure made of rows and columns that stores data inside a database.
A number a program uses to connect to a service on a computer. In MySQL, port 3306 is the usual number used to connect to the MySQL server.
Datatype refers to the type of data that a column is allowed to store. It determines what kind of values can be entered in that column, such as numbers, text, or dates.
A numeric datatype used to store whole numbers (integers) without decimal values.
A datatype used to store text of variable length, up to a specified number of characters.
A datatype used to store calendar dates in the format YYYY-MM-DD.
A column constraint that prevents a column from storing null (empty) values.
A column setting that automatically generates a unique number for each new row, increasing by 1 each time.
A single box in a table where a row and column intersect, used to store one value.
